items.title: RTX 4090 sent for repair is a sophisticated fake with laser-etched VRAM and core, 'This is the best scam I've ever seen' — Scammers pulled a factory-level job to sell a dud to unsuspecting customer
items.description: Northwest Repair was sent an Asus ROG Strix 4090 that had a dead core and memory chips with fake markings to make it look like the real thing. Unfortunately, there's no way of telling what these counterfeit parts actually were, but the main takeaway is just how good these scammers are getting as of late.

Subscribe to our newsletter Northwest Repair is back with another repair video, this time featuring an Asus ROG Strix RTX 4090 that came in after a customer bought it used from eBay, but it turned out to be one of the most sophisticated fakes we've ever seen. The GPU chip and the VRAM were all fake; it had been shaved down to remove the markings and then laser-etched with fake information.The seller claimed it was part of an Amazon pallet deal, but it turns out that the card was emblematic of a much larger scam. It wasn't being recognized in any test bench, and opening up the GPU revealed a rather clean-looking PCB that didn't show any obvious points of failure. That is, until you look closer to figure out its true nature. Nvidia 4090 PERFECT SCAM just hit the marker - YouTube Watch On This RTX 4090 was a sophisticated fake, the "perfect scam," as described in the video, because it's a completely empty GPU. The core and all of the VRAM chips are counterfeit; they've been shaved down to remove their original markings. The bad actors have then gone ahead and laser-engraved the chips with new markings that correspond to the actual thing, fooling even the experienced eye.Article continues below You may like eBay seller gets scammed as customer returns $4,000 RTX 5090 with missing GPU core and memory modules Modders use jumper wires and a custom BIOS to save a damaged RTX 4090 from the trash Flabbergasted GPU repair wizard highlights dangers of liquid metal after leak kills entire RTX 5070 Ti The core and the memory had correct part numbers and logos that would never look fake. There was no leftover flux on the board or any signs of baking that'd suggest it had been tampered with before. Even the compound around the core that's supposed to keep it in place and seal it electrically was all there. It's only when you start to dig deeper into the little details that the truth emerges.The aforementioned compound appeared a bit darker than usual, like it was epoxy that had yellowed after drying, but the repairman shrugged it off for an overclocker's odyssey. The first real giveaway came when the card was put under a microscope, where one of the solder pads had been wicked, so it looked visibly different from the neighboring ones.Further inspection revealed that the card had been perhaps washed ultrasonically because it had faint streak marks all over the PCB. When checking out the memory chips this closely, it was clear as day that they'd been shaved. The top layer had been physically rubbed off so that the scammers could put new GDDR6X markings on it, and they repeated the same process with the core that was likely replaced as well (given the epoxy).Hence, there's no saying what the core or VRAM actually is under that fabricated (no pun intended) signage. The core says "AD102-300-A1" as it would on a real RTX 4090, but it's not a real RTX 4090; hell, whatever it is, it doesn't even work. There were no other telltale clues that would make this fake stand out, which is extremely troubling. It shows just how good these fraudsters have gotten at emulating different GPUs.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe rest of the video serves as a cautionary tale, with the host constantly reminding people to remain vigilant during these times. The shortages birthed by the ongoing AI boom have made people desperate on both sides — the customers yearn for a good deal while the scammers know the market is vulnerable, and they can't leave a golden opportunity like that vacant. (Image credit: Northwest Repair on YouTube)"This is the best scam I've ever seen," says Northwest Repair when comparing a real RTX 3080 core to the fake one larping as an RTX 4090. Ada Lovelace GPUs have a slightly different layout for the MLCCs laid around the perimeter of the core. When you look at them side-by-side, it's pretty easy to distinguish between the two, but you'd need to know this beforehand to catch it. Also, it's not exactly practical to expect that a customer would open up their card to specifically check for this either.In the pinned comment, the YouTuber said that they don't think something like this could be pulled off by an individual or even a professional workshop. Instead, this is a factory-level job that likely stems from those illicit underground vendors that convert gaming GPUs to AI workhorses with VRAM upgrades. Another viewer jokingly remarked that perhaps this "factory" sent in the fake so they could identify what to improve for free.Too often, scams like these are just let go of. Northwest Repair recommends filing a police report so the perpetrators can be brought to justice through an official investigation. We've covered similar cases before, but we've never seen the scammers go to such lengths to hide a counterfeit. Usually, opening up the card immediately gives it away, but clearly, the grift is getting increasingly creative. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 送修的RTX 4090是精心制作的假货，显存和核心都经过激光蚀刻，“这是我见过的最好的骗局”——骗子们搞了个工厂级别的活儿，把废品卖给了毫无戒心的顾客
items.descriptionZh:
一家名为Northwest Repair的维修团队近日发布了一段令人震惊的拆解视频，揭露了一张从eBay购买的所谓“华硕ROG Strix RTX 4090”显卡竟然是迄今为止见过的最精密的仿冒品。这张显卡的买家通过二手平台购入，声称是来自亚马逊托盘交易的一部分，但实际背后隐藏着一个规模更大的骗局。视频中，这张卡在测试平台上完全无法被识别，而当维修专家打开外壳后，发现PCB表面看起来异常干净，没有任何明显的故障点——直到显微镜下的仔细观察才暴露了其真实面目。

这张RTX 4090被形容为“完美的骗局”，因为GPU核心和所有显存芯片都是假冒的。造假者将原始芯片的表面磨掉，去除原有的型号标记，然后通过激光蚀刻重新刻上对应真品的编号和Logo，以至于即使是经验丰富的维修人员也难以一眼识破。核心上的环氧树脂封胶颜色比正常略深，像是老化发黄，但起初被误认为是超频玩家的改装痕迹。真正的破绽出现在显微镜下：其中一个焊盘被“吸走”而显得与周边不同；整块PCB表面有淡淡的条纹，表明曾被超声波清洗过；检查显存芯片时，明显可以看到顶层被物理磨除，然后印上假的GDDR6X标识。核心上的字样写着“AD102-300-A1”——这正是真实RTX 4090的型号，但实际上它根本不是RTX 4090，甚至根本不能工作。

除了这些细节，整个假卡几乎没有其他明显破绽，这令人极为担忧——它表明造假者模拟不同GPU的能力已经达到了令人不安的高度。视频中，维修专家将这张假卡核心与一块真实的RTX 3080核心进行并排对比，发现Ada Lovelace架构的GPU在核心外围的MLCC（多层陶瓷电容）布局上有细微差别，只有事先知道这一特征的人才能区分。然而，要求普通消费者在购买后自行拆开显卡、用显微镜检查电容布局，显然不切实际。

在视频的置顶评论中，维修专家表示，这种级别的仿冒不太可能由个人或小型修理店完成，而是来自工厂级的作业流程，很可能源自那些将游戏显卡改装成AI重负载工作卡的非法地下作坊——它们通常会对GPU进行显存升级和重新标识。更有观众调侃说，也许这个“工厂”故意送修这张假卡，就是为了免费获得对手的识别技巧，以便进一步改进。

这起事件再次警示人们，当前AI热潮导致的显卡短缺，让买家和骗子都陷入了极端：消费者渴望“好价”，而欺诈分子深知市场脆弱、机会难得，纷纷使出更复杂的手段。这张假卡若不自用或仔细检查，极有可能蒙混过关。Northwest Repair建议受害者务必向警方报案，通过官方调查将造假者绳之以法。此前我们也报道过类似的假冒案件，但从未见过造假者如此费尽心机地隐藏破绽——通常打开外壳就能看出端倪，但显然，骗局正变得越来越“高明”。面对二手高端显卡交易，买家必须保持极度警惕。

items.title: AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D review: An unbeatable value gaming CPU
items.description: The Ryzen 7 9850X3D was never set up for success, but our testing reveals the ugly side of AMD’s latest X3D CPU: higher power consumption for minuscule performance gains.
items.content: We’ve had the Ryzen 5 7600X3D for a while, and it’s always placed highly in our CPU benchmark hierarchy, as well as shown up among the best CPUs for gaming. But we never gave the chip a proper review because it was only available to a select number of people. Launched in 2024 as a Micro Center exclusive, it’s always been an elusive chip, packing AMD’s coveted 3D V-Cache while arriving (initially) below $300. We’re taking a stab at reviewing it now because you can actually buy the Ryzen 5 7600X3D online.Micro Center recently started selling on Amazon, and although you’ll pay a premium versus in-store pricing ($240 compared to $200 at current prices), the Ryzen 5 7600X3D still represents an excellent value. It’s such a good value for gaming PCs that it’s worth an in-depth review a year and a half after it was released.AMD has moved on to its Zen 5 range, but we haven’t seen a replacement for the Ryzen 5 7600X3D yet, with most of the 3D V-Cache attention going toward higher-end SKUs like the Ryzen 7 9850X3D and Ryzen 9 9950X3D2. In the $200 to $250 price bracket, the Ryzen 5 7600X3D remains uncontested for gaming performance.Intel hasn’t sat idly by in the last 18 months, though. Although we saw the disappointing release of Arrow Lake, the new Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs from team blue are surprisingly potent. In particular, the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus puts up a fight against the Ryzen 5 7600X3D’s gaming prowess around that same $200 to $250 price bracket.Although the 7600X3D still represents a great value for gamers, it’s lost some of its edge in application performance. AMD’s X3D chips have never been the first choice for productivity, outside of a flagship like the Ryzen 9 9950X3D, but the X3D nerf, combined with a six-core CCD on a last-gen architecture, isn’t a great recipe for blazing application performance.We’ll cover both gaming and productivity in our in-depth benchmarking throughout this review, as well as look at power draw and efficiency to see where the Ryzen 5 7600X3D lands.AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D (AMD Ryzen 5) at Amazon for $229.99AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D specifications and pricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyCPU / (MSRP)Street PriceArchitectureCache (L2 + L3)Cores/Threads (P+E)Base/Boost Clock (GHz)TDP / PPTRyzen 9 7950X3D ($700)$650Zen 4 X3D144 MB16 / 324.2 / 5.7120W / 162W Ryzen 9 7950X ($700)Out of StockZen 480 MB16 / 324.5 / 5.7170W / 230WRyzen 7 7900X3D ($600)Out of StockZen 4 X3D140 MB12 / 244.4 / 5.6120W / 162WRyzen 9 7900X ($550)$299Zen 476 MB12 / 244.7 / 5.6170W / 230WRyzen 7 7800X3D ($450)$399Zen 4 X3D104 MB8 / 164.2 / 5120W / 162WRyzen 7 7700X ($400)$249Zen 440 MB8 / 164.5 / 5.4105W / 142WRyzen 5 7600X3D ($300)$246Zen 4 X3D102 MB6 / 124.1 / 4.765W / 88WRyzen 5 7600X ($300)$180Zen 438 MB6 / 124.7 / 5.3105W / 142WAMD’s second-gen 3D V-Cache with Zen 5 CPUs have spoiled gamers a bit, offering basically identical specs to the non-X3D parts, by placing the SRAM chunk under the die instead of on top of it. The Ryzen 5 7600X3D still keeps the original design with the extra SRAM on top, which acts as an insulating layer between the cores and the IHS. That means lower clock speeds, lower peak power, and no access to traditional multiplier-based overclocking, which is otherwise available on Zen 4 chips.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHere, the power is cut down dramatically, to a 65W TDP and 88W PPT. Chips like the Ryzen 7 7800X3D not only earned their stripes on the back of exceptional gaming performance, but also high efficiency. Zen 4 overall is very efficient, so although that 65W TDP limits the Ryzen 5 7600X3D in some non-gaming workloads, the extra cache is more than enough to make up for power shortcomings in games.The biggest victim of lower power is peak clock speed, with the Ryzen 5 7600X3D topping out 600 MHz lower than the base Ryzen 5 7600X. Peak clock speeds aren’t the biggest performance factor in most modern games, which is something we saw on full display with our recent Ryzen 7 9850X3D review.On the cache front, the 7600X3D packs in 102 MB total. That’s technically lower than the Ryzen 7 7800X3D, but not by much. Both chips have the same 96 MB SRAM chunk bonded to the top of the die, giving them both access to the same amount of shared L3 cache. The 2 MB haircut comes from the L2 cache located on the cores.MORE: Best CPU for gamingMORE: CPU Benchmark HierarchyMORE: Intel vs AMDMORE: How to Overclock a CPU AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D: Price Comparison $249.99 $229.99ViewLow Stock $299.99 $269.95View $298.49View $470.90ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by

items.titleZh: AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D 评测：一款无与伦比的超值游戏CPU
items.descriptionZh:
AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D 是一款自2024年发布以来就备受关注的游戏CPU，最初仅限Micro Center独家销售，因此评测一直空缺。如今该芯片已通过Amazon等渠道在线销售，尽管价格相比店内贵了约40美元（现价约$240，店内约$200），但其卓越的游戏性价比仍值得在发布一年半后进行深度评测。在200至250美元价位段，它依然是游戏性能的王者，AMD尚未推出Zen 5架构的替代品，而Intel的Core Ultra 5 250K Plus则成为其有力竞争者。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Ryzen 5 7600X3D基于Zen 4架构，采用第二代3D V-Cache技术，但设计方式与Zen 5的X3D芯片不同：它将额外的96 MB SRAM（总共102 MB缓存）堆叠在核心上方，而非下方。这种“堆叠式”结构虽然大幅增加了L3缓存（基础L3为32 MB，叠加后总计96 MB，加上6 MB L2，总缓存102 MB），但SRAM层充当了核心与IHS之间的隔热层，导致核心频率降低（基础4.1 GHz，加速4.7 GHz，比非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X低600 MHz），峰值功耗也受限（TDP仅65W，PPT 88W），且无法进行传统的倍频超频。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- 核心配置：6核12线程，频率4.1/4.7 GHz，缓存102 MB（L2+L3），TDP 65W。  
- 游戏性能：得益于大容量L3缓存，在多数现代游戏中表现优异，与更高端的Ryzen 7 7800X3D（8核16线程，104 MB缓存）差距不大，但功耗远低于后者（65W vs 120W）。相比非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X，游戏帧率有明显提升，尽管频率低600 MHz。  
- 生产应用性能：由于频率较低且仅6核心，在生产力任务中表现平庸，落后于同价位的Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus和非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X。不过，其高能效在一些轻负载场景下仍是优势。  
- 与竞品对比：在$200-$250价位，Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus（Arrow Lake Refresh）在游戏性能上能与之抗衡，但7600X3D仍保持领先；而在生产力方面，Intel芯片更占优。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
Ryzen 5 7600X3D的核心价值在于为预算有限但追求极致游戏体验的用户提供高性价比选择。它的3D V-Cache技术通过缓存优化大幅减少内存延迟，对缓存敏感的现代游戏（如《赛博朋克2077》、《文明6》等）提升显著。虽然无法超频，但65W TDP使其适合小型ITX或低功耗游戏主机。对于需要兼顾创作和游戏的用户，建议考虑更高核心数的X3D芯片（如Ryzen 7 7800X3D）或Intel平台。总体而言，它仍是当前最值得推荐的入门级游戏CPU之一，尤其适合主玩游戏、对功耗敏感且预算在2000元左右的玩家。

items.title: Commodore backs down over FPGA firmware lockdown — firm stops trying to block third-party firmware installs but will stand firm against bricked modded units
items.description: Iconic home computer brand has reversed its no-third-party firmware decision but makes it clear that users will get 'no free support/replacement for bricked modded units.'
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Commodore fans were split earlier this month as an official document revealed the firm planned to prevent tinkerers from loading third-party firmware to the C64 Ultimate computer by locking down such upgrades to the FPGA. The iconic home computer brand has now backed down, reversing the decision but issuing a statement making it clear that users can enjoy “free experimentation, just no free support/replacement for bricked modded units.”Last week we said we were considering restricting non-Commodore FPGA firmware on the Commodore 64 Ultimate, to avoid Commodore covering free support or replacements for machines damaged by unsupported mods - basically our version of a bricked jailbroken iPhone.We listened. We… pic.twitter.com/gCMk9vNEofApril 23, 2026It is evident that the FPGA firmware gatekeeping decision had been a tricky one for the new Commodore. And telegraphing a change “preventing firmware not released by Commodore from being loaded onto the hardware,” split the user base, but we think most comments were on the side of user freedom. As social media and forum discussions heated up on the topic, Commodore published a detailed blog with a point–by-point defense of its new policy.To be clear, the FPGA firmware flashing blocks were not rolled out yet. The 1.1.0 firmware release simply came with a statement that “A future update may introduce safeguards to help make sure incompatible firmware not released by Commodore does not damage your motherboard.” In the follow-up blog the seminal home computing firm highlighted that it had already seen non-functioning casualties of third-party firmware updates requiring support. This wasn’t sustainable, it reasoned. Servicing “hardware returns and replacements due to actions entirely out of our control” was a step beyond what Commodore was prepared to do.Article continues below You may like Commodore fans split over C64 Ultimate FPGA firmware lockdown Denuvo promises countermeasures against the recent hypervisor-based DRM bypasses Jim Keller's Tenstorrent is downgrading Blackhole p150 cards from 140 to 120 tensor cores via firmware update The new Commodore “values freedom of choice the most.”The official blog post update from Commodore regarding firmware is pretty clear about the change in policy. “We will not prevent you from installing other firmware on your Commodore 64 Ultimate,” states a key highlighted phrase in the post.It goes on to say it remains worried about users bricking their C64U machines and then putting in return and replacement requests (under warranty, we guess). However, it admits “that's 'Ultimately' an "us" problem, not a "you" problem.”In the firmware block policy's place, there will instead be a stern disclaimer. Commodore will insist that “community-installed firmware, patches, or other modifications are used at the owner’s own risk, and Commodore cannot provide free support, free warranty service, or free replacement for units bricked or damaged as a result.” Most retro gaming community and tinkerer folks will probably be pleased with Commodore gracefully bowing to popular pressure on this. It will be interesting to see how the new policy affects the warranty returns process. items.titleZh: Commodore在FPGA固件锁定问题上让步——公司不再阻止第三方固件安装，但将坚守对变砖改装设备的立场
items.descriptionZh:
Commodore公司近期在C64 Ultimate（C64U）电脑上引发的FPGA固件封锁风波，最终以品牌方妥协告一段落。这家标志性的家用电脑品牌曾在官方文档中宣布计划阻止用户加载第三方固件，通过锁定FPGA升级来限制“非官方”修改。这一决定在粉丝群体中引发了强烈分歧，但经过一轮公开讨论和舆论压力后，Commodore最终放弃了封锁计划，转而发布声明，明确用户“可以自由实验，只是不能因改装导致的‘变砖’问题获得免费支持或更换”。

背景上，C64 Ultimate是Commodore推出的一款现代复刻版家用电脑，基于FPGA（现场可编程门阵列）技术实现硬件仿真，允许用户加载不同固件来运行各种游戏或系统。FPGA固件的核心原理是：通过重新配置芯片内部的逻辑门电路，可以模拟不同的硬件架构（如原始C64、C128等），从而提供灵活的兼容性。Commodore最初担心，第三方固件可能与硬件设计不兼容，导致主板损坏（即“变砖”），而用户如果因此提出退货或保修更换，公司将承担无法控制的成本。因此，他们在1.1.0版固件更新中附带声明：“未来更新可能引入安全措施，以防止非Commodore发布的固件损害主板。”

然而，这一预告立刻遭到了社区和改装爱好者的强烈反对。在社交媒体和论坛上，多数用户认为自由使用硬件是基本权利，品牌不应以安全为由限制用户选择。Commodore随后发布了一篇更详细的博客文章，最初试图逐条辩护自己的政策，称已经收到多起因第三方固件导致机器损坏并要求支持的案例，认为这种“因完全超出我们控制的行为而承担硬件退货和更换”的做法不可持续。

但在舆论压力下，Commodore最终改变了立场。其更新的官方博客明确写道：“我们不会阻止你在你的Commodore 64 Ultimate上安装其他固件。” 公司承认，虽然仍担心用户因改装变砖后提出更换请求（可能在保修期内），但“这最终是‘我们’的问题，而不是‘你们’的问题”。取而代之的，Commodore将采取更严格的免责声明政策：用户需要明确知晓“社区安装的固件、补丁或其他修改完全由所有者自行承担风险，Commodore不提供免费支持、免费保修服务或因变砖或损坏导致的免费更换。”

从技术层面看，此次争执的核心在于FPGA固件的可编程特性。FPGA本身允许用户自由加载比特流文件来改变芯片功能，这是其强大灵活性的来源。Commodore原本计划通过数字签名或其他验证机制锁定FPGA，只允许加载官方签名的固件，从而防止用户“瞎折腾”。但这一做法在技术社区看来，违背了FPGA开放的初衷——就像限制一台现代PC只能运行官方操作系统一样不可接受。最终，Commodore选择了更温和的方案：保留技术上的开放性，但通过法律声明划分责任边界。

性能参数方面，C64 Ultimate本身并未因这次风波改变任何硬件规格。它依然基于FPGA架构，能够精确模拟C64、C128等经典系统，并提供现代连接接口（如HDMI、USB、SD卡）和扩展功能。其FPGA固件更新通常带来新的兼容性或功能改进，而第三方固件则可能提供定制化的模拟体验或特殊功能。

这一事件对行业和用户社区产生了显著影响。首先，它再次凸显了“用户自主权”与“品牌责任”之间的经典矛盾。对于复古计算爱好者而言，改装和实验是文化的一部分，任何限制都被视为对自由精神的背叛。Commodore的妥协被认为是“明智之举”，因为它保持了品牌在核心用户群中的好感度。其次，新的免责政策意味着，未来用户一旦因第三方程式的固件导致硬件损坏，将无法享受免费保修或更换。这对动手能力较强的用户影响不大，但对于普通消费者而言，需要更谨慎地评估风险。

从应用场景来看，C64 Ultimate本身就是为怀旧玩家、复古计算爱好者以及技术发烧友设计的。它既可以作为一台原汁原味的C64兼容机来玩经典游戏，也可以作为一个开放的硬件平台来实验FPGA技术。Commodore本次的决策，实际上承认了这种开放性的核心价值，同时将支持责任与用户行为解耦。

总体而言，这场“固件封锁”风波以用户获得胜利告终。Commodore虽然退让，但也明确了边界：用户自由实验的权利被保留，但由此产生的一切后果自负。这种既保护品牌利益又尊重社区文化的折中方案，很可能成为未来类似争议的参考范例。

items.title: Taiwan's stock market surpasses the UK's despite having less than a quarter of the UK's economy — AI boom propels Taiwan forward, TSMC alone accounts for more than 40% of Taiwan's total market value
items.description: Taiwan's stock market is now worth more than the United Kingdom's, in a shift driven almost entirely by the insatiable global appetite for AI chips.

Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwan's stock market is now worth more than the United Kingdom's, according to recent Bloomberg data, in a shift driven almost entirely by the insatiable global appetite for AI chips. The island's listed companies carry a combined valuation of roughly $4.3 trillion, edging past Europe's largest equity market, despite Taiwan's economy being less than a quarter the size of the UK's. South Korea, powered by Samsung and SK hynix, is roughly $140 billion behind and closing fast.As for Taiwan’s stock market constituents, TSMC makes up the bulk, with a market cap of approximately US$1.98 trillion, or 40% of Taiwan’s entire stock market value. No other major economy has this degree of single-company dependence. By comparison, Apple represents roughly 7% of the S&P 500.On Thursday, Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission raised the single-stock investment cap for local equity funds from 10% to 25% of a fund's net asset value. The change applies only to companies whose market weighting exceeds 10%, a threshold TSMC alone meets. That adjustment triggered a 4.3% jump in TSMC shares on Friday and pushed the benchmark TAIEX up 2.7%, according to Taiwanese media.Article continues below You may like AMD's market cap hits all-time high, Intel hits 25-year high on Agentic AI's insatiable demand for CPUs Global semiconductor foundry market hit a record $320 billion in 2025 as TSMC pulled further ahead Ongoing trade war has TSMC and Taiwan stuck between a rock and a hard place TSMC reported record first-quarter earnings last week, with net income up 40.6% year-over-year to roughly $18 billion. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to more than 30% growth and confirmed plans to add another 3nm fab to meet AI demand, which it expects to outstrip supply through 2027.South Korea's market, KOSPI, has already overtaken both Germany and France this year and is up 44% in 2026. Samsung and SK hynix account for nearly half the index's total weighting. The former’s share price has nearly quadrupled since the start of 2025, while SK hynix has risen roughly sixfold over the same period. These gains reflect the ongoing memory supercycle, with demand for HBM far exceeding current production capacity.William Bratton, head of cash equity research for Asia-Pacific at BNP Paribas, told the Financial Times that, aside from ASML, European-listed equities have almost no exposure to the current AI hardware buildout. He added that South Korea could overtake the UK as well if the current rally continues.The disparity between these semiconductor-heavy Asian markets and Europe highlights how narrowly the financial benefits of the AI infrastructure boom are distributed. Taiwan's GDP is roughly $977 billion, according to IMF estimates, versus the UK's $4.3 trillion. While the gap between those two economies hasn’t changed, the gap between their stock markets has.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 台湾股市超越英国，尽管经济规模不到英国的四分之一——AI热潮推动台湾前进，台积电一家就占台湾总市值40%以上
items.descriptionZh:
台湾股市市值近日首次超越英国，成为全球资本市场格局变化的一个重要里程碑。根据彭博数据，台湾上市公司总市值已达到约4.3万亿美元，略高于欧洲最大股票市场英国。而这一转变几乎完全由全球对人工智能（AI）芯片的旺盛需求推动。尽管台湾经济规模仅为英国的四分之一（根据IMF估算，台湾GDP约9770亿美元，英国为4.3万亿美元），但其股市市值却已反超。与此同时，韩国股市（KOSPI）正在迅速追赶，目前仅落后约1400亿美元，且因三星电子和SK海力士的强劲表现，差距正在快速缩小。

此次台湾股市的主导力量是台积电（TSMC）。台积电市值约1.98万亿美元，占台湾股市总市值的40%，这一集中度在全球主要经济体中绝无仅有。作为对比，苹果公司在美国标普500指数中的权重仅约7%。本周四，台湾金融监督管理委员会将本地股票基金对单只股票的投资上限从基金净值的10%提高至25%，该调整仅适用于市值权重超过10%的公司——目前只有台积电满足条件。这一政策变化推动台积电股价于周五上涨4.3%，并带动台湾加权指数（TAIEX）上升2.7%。

台积电在上一周公布了创纪录的第一季度财报：净利润同比增长40.6%，达到约180亿美元。公司已将全年营收增长预期上调至超过30%，并确认计划新增一座3纳米晶圆厂以满足AI需求。台积电预计AI芯片需求将持续超出供应能力，至少到2027年。

韩国方面，KOSPI指数今年已超越德国和法国股市，2026年以来累计上涨44%。三星电子和SK海力士合计占该指数权重近一半。前者股价自2025年初以来已上涨近四倍，同期后者涨幅约六倍。这种增长反映了当前的内存超级周期，尤其是高带宽存储器（HBM）的需求远超出当前产能。

法国巴黎银行亚太现金股票研究主管William Bratton对《金融时报》表示，除ASML外，欧洲上市企业几乎未参与当前AI硬件建设的热潮。他补充说，如果目前涨势持续，韩国股市也可能超越英国。这些半导体密集型亚洲市场与欧洲之间的巨大差异，凸显了AI基础设施繁荣带来的金融收益分布极为不均——尽管台湾与英国的经济规模差距未变，但两者股市的市值鸿沟已经逆转。

history.title: RTX 4090 sent for repair is a sophisticated fake with laser-etched VRAM and core, 'This is the best scam I've ever seen' — Scammers pulled a factory-level job to sell a dud to unsuspecting customer
history.description: Northwest Repair was sent an Asus ROG Strix 4090 that had a dead core and memory chips with fake markings to make it look like the real thing. Unfortunately, there's no way of telling what these counterfeit parts actually were, but the main takeaway is just how good these scammers are getting as of late.

Subscribe to our newsletter Northwest Repair is back with another repair video, this time featuring an Asus ROG Strix RTX 4090 that came in after a customer bought it used from eBay, but it turned out to be one of the most sophisticated fakes we've ever seen. The GPU chip and the VRAM were all fake; it had been shaved down to remove the markings and then laser-etched with fake information.The seller claimed it was part of an Amazon pallet deal, but it turns out that the card was emblematic of a much larger scam. It wasn't being recognized in any test bench, and opening up the GPU revealed a rather clean-looking PCB that didn't show any obvious points of failure. That is, until you look closer to figure out its true nature. Nvidia 4090 PERFECT SCAM just hit the marker - YouTube Watch On This RTX 4090 was a sophisticated fake, the "perfect scam," as described in the video, because it's a completely empty GPU. The core and all of the VRAM chips are counterfeit; they've been shaved down to remove their original markings. The bad actors have then gone ahead and laser-engraved the chips with new markings that correspond to the actual thing, fooling even the experienced eye.Article continues below You may like eBay seller gets scammed as customer returns $4,000 RTX 5090 with missing GPU core and memory modules Modders use jumper wires and a custom BIOS to save a damaged RTX 4090 from the trash Flabbergasted GPU repair wizard highlights dangers of liquid metal after leak kills entire RTX 5070 Ti The core and the memory had correct part numbers and logos that would never look fake. There was no leftover flux on the board or any signs of baking that'd suggest it had been tampered with before. Even the compound around the core that's supposed to keep it in place and seal it electrically was all there. It's only when you start to dig deeper into the little details that the truth emerges.The aforementioned compound appeared a bit darker than usual, like it was epoxy that had yellowed after drying, but the repairman shrugged it off for an overclocker's odyssey. The first real giveaway came when the card was put under a microscope, where one of the solder pads had been wicked, so it looked visibly different from the neighboring ones.Further inspection revealed that the card had been perhaps washed ultrasonically because it had faint streak marks all over the PCB. When checking out the memory chips this closely, it was clear as day that they'd been shaved. The top layer had been physically rubbed off so that the scammers could put new GDDR6X markings on it, and they repeated the same process with the core that was likely replaced as well (given the epoxy).Hence, there's no saying what the core or VRAM actually is under that fabricated (no pun intended) signage. The core says "AD102-300-A1" as it would on a real RTX 4090, but it's not a real RTX 4090; hell, whatever it is, it doesn't even work. There were no other telltale clues that would make this fake stand out, which is extremely troubling. It shows just how good these fraudsters have gotten at emulating different GPUs.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe rest of the video serves as a cautionary tale, with the host constantly reminding people to remain vigilant during these times. The shortages birthed by the ongoing AI boom have made people desperate on both sides — the customers yearn for a good deal while the scammers know the market is vulnerable, and they can't leave a golden opportunity like that vacant. (Image credit: Northwest Repair on YouTube)"This is the best scam I've ever seen," says Northwest Repair when comparing a real RTX 3080 core to the fake one larping as an RTX 4090. Ada Lovelace GPUs have a slightly different layout for the MLCCs laid around the perimeter of the core. When you look at them side-by-side, it's pretty easy to distinguish between the two, but you'd need to know this beforehand to catch it. Also, it's not exactly practical to expect that a customer would open up their card to specifically check for this either.In the pinned comment, the YouTuber said that they don't think something like this could be pulled off by an individual or even a professional workshop. Instead, this is a factory-level job that likely stems from those illicit underground vendors that convert gaming GPUs to AI workhorses with VRAM upgrades. Another viewer jokingly remarked that perhaps this "factory" sent in the fake so they could identify what to improve for free.Too often, scams like these are just let go of. Northwest Repair recommends filing a police report so the perpetrators can be brought to justice through an official investigation. We've covered similar cases before, but we've never seen the scammers go to such lengths to hide a counterfeit. Usually, opening up the card immediately gives it away, but clearly, the grift is getting increasingly creative. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 送修的RTX 4090是精心制作的假货，显存和核心都经过激光蚀刻，“这是我见过的最好的骗局”——骗子们搞了个工厂级别的活儿，把废品卖给了毫无戒心的顾客
history.descriptionZh:
一家名为Northwest Repair的维修团队近日发布了一段令人震惊的拆解视频，揭露了一张从eBay购买的所谓“华硕ROG Strix RTX 4090”显卡竟然是迄今为止见过的最精密的仿冒品。这张显卡的买家通过二手平台购入，声称是来自亚马逊托盘交易的一部分，但实际背后隐藏着一个规模更大的骗局。视频中，这张卡在测试平台上完全无法被识别，而当维修专家打开外壳后，发现PCB表面看起来异常干净，没有任何明显的故障点——直到显微镜下的仔细观察才暴露了其真实面目。

这张RTX 4090被形容为“完美的骗局”，因为GPU核心和所有显存芯片都是假冒的。造假者将原始芯片的表面磨掉，去除原有的型号标记，然后通过激光蚀刻重新刻上对应真品的编号和Logo，以至于即使是经验丰富的维修人员也难以一眼识破。核心上的环氧树脂封胶颜色比正常略深，像是老化发黄，但起初被误认为是超频玩家的改装痕迹。真正的破绽出现在显微镜下：其中一个焊盘被“吸走”而显得与周边不同；整块PCB表面有淡淡的条纹，表明曾被超声波清洗过；检查显存芯片时，明显可以看到顶层被物理磨除，然后印上假的GDDR6X标识。核心上的字样写着“AD102-300-A1”——这正是真实RTX 4090的型号，但实际上它根本不是RTX 4090，甚至根本不能工作。

除了这些细节，整个假卡几乎没有其他明显破绽，这令人极为担忧——它表明造假者模拟不同GPU的能力已经达到了令人不安的高度。视频中，维修专家将这张假卡核心与一块真实的RTX 3080核心进行并排对比，发现Ada Lovelace架构的GPU在核心外围的MLCC（多层陶瓷电容）布局上有细微差别，只有事先知道这一特征的人才能区分。然而，要求普通消费者在购买后自行拆开显卡、用显微镜检查电容布局，显然不切实际。

在视频的置顶评论中，维修专家表示，这种级别的仿冒不太可能由个人或小型修理店完成，而是来自工厂级的作业流程，很可能源自那些将游戏显卡改装成AI重负载工作卡的非法地下作坊——它们通常会对GPU进行显存升级和重新标识。更有观众调侃说，也许这个“工厂”故意送修这张假卡，就是为了免费获得对手的识别技巧，以便进一步改进。

这起事件再次警示人们，当前AI热潮导致的显卡短缺，让买家和骗子都陷入了极端：消费者渴望“好价”，而欺诈分子深知市场脆弱、机会难得，纷纷使出更复杂的手段。这张假卡若不自用或仔细检查，极有可能蒙混过关。Northwest Repair建议受害者务必向警方报案，通过官方调查将造假者绳之以法。此前我们也报道过类似的假冒案件，但从未见过造假者如此费尽心机地隐藏破绽——通常打开外壳就能看出端倪，但显然，骗局正变得越来越“高明”。面对二手高端显卡交易，买家必须保持极度警惕。

history.title: AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D review: An unbeatable value gaming CPU
history.description: The Ryzen 7 9850X3D was never set up for success, but our testing reveals the ugly side of AMD’s latest X3D CPU: higher power consumption for minuscule performance gains.
history.content: We’ve had the Ryzen 5 7600X3D for a while, and it’s always placed highly in our CPU benchmark hierarchy, as well as shown up among the best CPUs for gaming. But we never gave the chip a proper review because it was only available to a select number of people. Launched in 2024 as a Micro Center exclusive, it’s always been an elusive chip, packing AMD’s coveted 3D V-Cache while arriving (initially) below $300. We’re taking a stab at reviewing it now because you can actually buy the Ryzen 5 7600X3D online.Micro Center recently started selling on Amazon, and although you’ll pay a premium versus in-store pricing ($240 compared to $200 at current prices), the Ryzen 5 7600X3D still represents an excellent value. It’s such a good value for gaming PCs that it’s worth an in-depth review a year and a half after it was released.AMD has moved on to its Zen 5 range, but we haven’t seen a replacement for the Ryzen 5 7600X3D yet, with most of the 3D V-Cache attention going toward higher-end SKUs like the Ryzen 7 9850X3D and Ryzen 9 9950X3D2. In the $200 to $250 price bracket, the Ryzen 5 7600X3D remains uncontested for gaming performance.Intel hasn’t sat idly by in the last 18 months, though. Although we saw the disappointing release of Arrow Lake, the new Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs from team blue are surprisingly potent. In particular, the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus puts up a fight against the Ryzen 5 7600X3D’s gaming prowess around that same $200 to $250 price bracket.Although the 7600X3D still represents a great value for gamers, it’s lost some of its edge in application performance. AMD’s X3D chips have never been the first choice for productivity, outside of a flagship like the Ryzen 9 9950X3D, but the X3D nerf, combined with a six-core CCD on a last-gen architecture, isn’t a great recipe for blazing application performance.We’ll cover both gaming and productivity in our in-depth benchmarking throughout this review, as well as look at power draw and efficiency to see where the Ryzen 5 7600X3D lands.AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D (AMD Ryzen 5) at Amazon for $229.99AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D specifications and pricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyCPU / (MSRP)Street PriceArchitectureCache (L2 + L3)Cores/Threads (P+E)Base/Boost Clock (GHz)TDP / PPTRyzen 9 7950X3D ($700)$650Zen 4 X3D144 MB16 / 324.2 / 5.7120W / 162W Ryzen 9 7950X ($700)Out of StockZen 480 MB16 / 324.5 / 5.7170W / 230WRyzen 7 7900X3D ($600)Out of StockZen 4 X3D140 MB12 / 244.4 / 5.6120W / 162WRyzen 9 7900X ($550)$299Zen 476 MB12 / 244.7 / 5.6170W / 230WRyzen 7 7800X3D ($450)$399Zen 4 X3D104 MB8 / 164.2 / 5120W / 162WRyzen 7 7700X ($400)$249Zen 440 MB8 / 164.5 / 5.4105W / 142WRyzen 5 7600X3D ($300)$246Zen 4 X3D102 MB6 / 124.1 / 4.765W / 88WRyzen 5 7600X ($300)$180Zen 438 MB6 / 124.7 / 5.3105W / 142WAMD’s second-gen 3D V-Cache with Zen 5 CPUs have spoiled gamers a bit, offering basically identical specs to the non-X3D parts, by placing the SRAM chunk under the die instead of on top of it. The Ryzen 5 7600X3D still keeps the original design with the extra SRAM on top, which acts as an insulating layer between the cores and the IHS. That means lower clock speeds, lower peak power, and no access to traditional multiplier-based overclocking, which is otherwise available on Zen 4 chips.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHere, the power is cut down dramatically, to a 65W TDP and 88W PPT. Chips like the Ryzen 7 7800X3D not only earned their stripes on the back of exceptional gaming performance, but also high efficiency. Zen 4 overall is very efficient, so although that 65W TDP limits the Ryzen 5 7600X3D in some non-gaming workloads, the extra cache is more than enough to make up for power shortcomings in games.The biggest victim of lower power is peak clock speed, with the Ryzen 5 7600X3D topping out 600 MHz lower than the base Ryzen 5 7600X. Peak clock speeds aren’t the biggest performance factor in most modern games, which is something we saw on full display with our recent Ryzen 7 9850X3D review.On the cache front, the 7600X3D packs in 102 MB total. That’s technically lower than the Ryzen 7 7800X3D, but not by much. Both chips have the same 96 MB SRAM chunk bonded to the top of the die, giving them both access to the same amount of shared L3 cache. The 2 MB haircut comes from the L2 cache located on the cores.MORE: Best CPU for gamingMORE: CPU Benchmark HierarchyMORE: Intel vs AMDMORE: How to Overclock a CPU AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D: Price Comparison $249.99 $229.99ViewLow Stock $299.99 $269.95View $298.49View $470.90ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D 评测：一款无与伦比的超值游戏CPU
history.descriptionZh:
AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D 是一款自2024年发布以来就备受关注的游戏CPU，最初仅限Micro Center独家销售，因此评测一直空缺。如今该芯片已通过Amazon等渠道在线销售，尽管价格相比店内贵了约40美元（现价约$240，店内约$200），但其卓越的游戏性价比仍值得在发布一年半后进行深度评测。在200至250美元价位段，它依然是游戏性能的王者，AMD尚未推出Zen 5架构的替代品，而Intel的Core Ultra 5 250K Plus则成为其有力竞争者。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Ryzen 5 7600X3D基于Zen 4架构，采用第二代3D V-Cache技术，但设计方式与Zen 5的X3D芯片不同：它将额外的96 MB SRAM（总共102 MB缓存）堆叠在核心上方，而非下方。这种“堆叠式”结构虽然大幅增加了L3缓存（基础L3为32 MB，叠加后总计96 MB，加上6 MB L2，总缓存102 MB），但SRAM层充当了核心与IHS之间的隔热层，导致核心频率降低（基础4.1 GHz，加速4.7 GHz，比非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X低600 MHz），峰值功耗也受限（TDP仅65W，PPT 88W），且无法进行传统的倍频超频。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- 核心配置：6核12线程，频率4.1/4.7 GHz，缓存102 MB（L2+L3），TDP 65W。  
- 游戏性能：得益于大容量L3缓存，在多数现代游戏中表现优异，与更高端的Ryzen 7 7800X3D（8核16线程，104 MB缓存）差距不大，但功耗远低于后者（65W vs 120W）。相比非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X，游戏帧率有明显提升，尽管频率低600 MHz。  
- 生产应用性能：由于频率较低且仅6核心，在生产力任务中表现平庸，落后于同价位的Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus和非X3D的Ryzen 5 7600X。不过，其高能效在一些轻负载场景下仍是优势。  
- 与竞品对比：在$200-$250价位，Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus（Arrow Lake Refresh）在游戏性能上能与之抗衡，但7600X3D仍保持领先；而在生产力方面，Intel芯片更占优。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
Ryzen 5 7600X3D的核心价值在于为预算有限但追求极致游戏体验的用户提供高性价比选择。它的3D V-Cache技术通过缓存优化大幅减少内存延迟，对缓存敏感的现代游戏（如《赛博朋克2077》、《文明6》等）提升显著。虽然无法超频，但65W TDP使其适合小型ITX或低功耗游戏主机。对于需要兼顾创作和游戏的用户，建议考虑更高核心数的X3D芯片（如Ryzen 7 7800X3D）或Intel平台。总体而言，它仍是当前最值得推荐的入门级游戏CPU之一，尤其适合主玩游戏、对功耗敏感且预算在2000元左右的玩家。

history.title: Commodore backs down over FPGA firmware lockdown — firm stops trying to block third-party firmware installs but will stand firm against bricked modded units
history.description: Iconic home computer brand has reversed its no-third-party firmware decision but makes it clear that users will get 'no free support/replacement for bricked modded units.'
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Commodore fans were split earlier this month as an official document revealed the firm planned to prevent tinkerers from loading third-party firmware to the C64 Ultimate computer by locking down such upgrades to the FPGA. The iconic home computer brand has now backed down, reversing the decision but issuing a statement making it clear that users can enjoy “free experimentation, just no free support/replacement for bricked modded units.”Last week we said we were considering restricting non-Commodore FPGA firmware on the Commodore 64 Ultimate, to avoid Commodore covering free support or replacements for machines damaged by unsupported mods - basically our version of a bricked jailbroken iPhone.We listened. We… pic.twitter.com/gCMk9vNEofApril 23, 2026It is evident that the FPGA firmware gatekeeping decision had been a tricky one for the new Commodore. And telegraphing a change “preventing firmware not released by Commodore from being loaded onto the hardware,” split the user base, but we think most comments were on the side of user freedom. As social media and forum discussions heated up on the topic, Commodore published a detailed blog with a point–by-point defense of its new policy.To be clear, the FPGA firmware flashing blocks were not rolled out yet. The 1.1.0 firmware release simply came with a statement that “A future update may introduce safeguards to help make sure incompatible firmware not released by Commodore does not damage your motherboard.” In the follow-up blog the seminal home computing firm highlighted that it had already seen non-functioning casualties of third-party firmware updates requiring support. This wasn’t sustainable, it reasoned. Servicing “hardware returns and replacements due to actions entirely out of our control” was a step beyond what Commodore was prepared to do.Article continues below You may like Commodore fans split over C64 Ultimate FPGA firmware lockdown Denuvo promises countermeasures against the recent hypervisor-based DRM bypasses Jim Keller's Tenstorrent is downgrading Blackhole p150 cards from 140 to 120 tensor cores via firmware update The new Commodore “values freedom of choice the most.”The official blog post update from Commodore regarding firmware is pretty clear about the change in policy. “We will not prevent you from installing other firmware on your Commodore 64 Ultimate,” states a key highlighted phrase in the post.It goes on to say it remains worried about users bricking their C64U machines and then putting in return and replacement requests (under warranty, we guess). However, it admits “that's 'Ultimately' an "us" problem, not a "you" problem.”In the firmware block policy's place, there will instead be a stern disclaimer. Commodore will insist that “community-installed firmware, patches, or other modifications are used at the owner’s own risk, and Commodore cannot provide free support, free warranty service, or free replacement for units bricked or damaged as a result.” Most retro gaming community and tinkerer folks will probably be pleased with Commodore gracefully bowing to popular pressure on this. It will be interesting to see how the new policy affects the warranty returns process. history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: Commodore在FPGA固件锁定问题上让步——公司不再阻止第三方固件安装，但将坚守对变砖改装设备的立场
history.descriptionZh:
Commodore公司近期在C64 Ultimate（C64U）电脑上引发的FPGA固件封锁风波，最终以品牌方妥协告一段落。这家标志性的家用电脑品牌曾在官方文档中宣布计划阻止用户加载第三方固件，通过锁定FPGA升级来限制“非官方”修改。这一决定在粉丝群体中引发了强烈分歧，但经过一轮公开讨论和舆论压力后，Commodore最终放弃了封锁计划，转而发布声明，明确用户“可以自由实验，只是不能因改装导致的‘变砖’问题获得免费支持或更换”。

背景上，C64 Ultimate是Commodore推出的一款现代复刻版家用电脑，基于FPGA（现场可编程门阵列）技术实现硬件仿真，允许用户加载不同固件来运行各种游戏或系统。FPGA固件的核心原理是：通过重新配置芯片内部的逻辑门电路，可以模拟不同的硬件架构（如原始C64、C128等），从而提供灵活的兼容性。Commodore最初担心，第三方固件可能与硬件设计不兼容，导致主板损坏（即“变砖”），而用户如果因此提出退货或保修更换，公司将承担无法控制的成本。因此，他们在1.1.0版固件更新中附带声明：“未来更新可能引入安全措施，以防止非Commodore发布的固件损害主板。”

然而，这一预告立刻遭到了社区和改装爱好者的强烈反对。在社交媒体和论坛上，多数用户认为自由使用硬件是基本权利，品牌不应以安全为由限制用户选择。Commodore随后发布了一篇更详细的博客文章，最初试图逐条辩护自己的政策，称已经收到多起因第三方固件导致机器损坏并要求支持的案例，认为这种“因完全超出我们控制的行为而承担硬件退货和更换”的做法不可持续。

但在舆论压力下，Commodore最终改变了立场。其更新的官方博客明确写道：“我们不会阻止你在你的Commodore 64 Ultimate上安装其他固件。” 公司承认，虽然仍担心用户因改装变砖后提出更换请求（可能在保修期内），但“这最终是‘我们’的问题，而不是‘你们’的问题”。取而代之的，Commodore将采取更严格的免责声明政策：用户需要明确知晓“社区安装的固件、补丁或其他修改完全由所有者自行承担风险，Commodore不提供免费支持、免费保修服务或因变砖或损坏导致的免费更换。”

从技术层面看，此次争执的核心在于FPGA固件的可编程特性。FPGA本身允许用户自由加载比特流文件来改变芯片功能，这是其强大灵活性的来源。Commodore原本计划通过数字签名或其他验证机制锁定FPGA，只允许加载官方签名的固件，从而防止用户“瞎折腾”。但这一做法在技术社区看来，违背了FPGA开放的初衷——就像限制一台现代PC只能运行官方操作系统一样不可接受。最终，Commodore选择了更温和的方案：保留技术上的开放性，但通过法律声明划分责任边界。

性能参数方面，C64 Ultimate本身并未因这次风波改变任何硬件规格。它依然基于FPGA架构，能够精确模拟C64、C128等经典系统，并提供现代连接接口（如HDMI、USB、SD卡）和扩展功能。其FPGA固件更新通常带来新的兼容性或功能改进，而第三方固件则可能提供定制化的模拟体验或特殊功能。

这一事件对行业和用户社区产生了显著影响。首先，它再次凸显了“用户自主权”与“品牌责任”之间的经典矛盾。对于复古计算爱好者而言，改装和实验是文化的一部分，任何限制都被视为对自由精神的背叛。Commodore的妥协被认为是“明智之举”，因为它保持了品牌在核心用户群中的好感度。其次，新的免责政策意味着，未来用户一旦因第三方程式的固件导致硬件损坏，将无法享受免费保修或更换。这对动手能力较强的用户影响不大，但对于普通消费者而言，需要更谨慎地评估风险。

从应用场景来看，C64 Ultimate本身就是为怀旧玩家、复古计算爱好者以及技术发烧友设计的。它既可以作为一台原汁原味的C64兼容机来玩经典游戏，也可以作为一个开放的硬件平台来实验FPGA技术。Commodore本次的决策，实际上承认了这种开放性的核心价值，同时将支持责任与用户行为解耦。

总体而言，这场“固件封锁”风波以用户获得胜利告终。Commodore虽然退让，但也明确了边界：用户自由实验的权利被保留，但由此产生的一切后果自负。这种既保护品牌利益又尊重社区文化的折中方案，很可能成为未来类似争议的参考范例。

history.title: Taiwan's stock market surpasses the UK's despite having less than a quarter of the UK's economy — AI boom propels Taiwan forward, TSMC alone accounts for more than 40% of Taiwan's total market value
history.description: Taiwan's stock market is now worth more than the United Kingdom's, in a shift driven almost entirely by the insatiable global appetite for AI chips.

Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwan's stock market is now worth more than the United Kingdom's, according to recent Bloomberg data, in a shift driven almost entirely by the insatiable global appetite for AI chips. The island's listed companies carry a combined valuation of roughly $4.3 trillion, edging past Europe's largest equity market, despite Taiwan's economy being less than a quarter the size of the UK's. South Korea, powered by Samsung and SK hynix, is roughly $140 billion behind and closing fast.As for Taiwan’s stock market constituents, TSMC makes up the bulk, with a market cap of approximately US$1.98 trillion, or 40% of Taiwan’s entire stock market value. No other major economy has this degree of single-company dependence. By comparison, Apple represents roughly 7% of the S&P 500.On Thursday, Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission raised the single-stock investment cap for local equity funds from 10% to 25% of a fund's net asset value. The change applies only to companies whose market weighting exceeds 10%, a threshold TSMC alone meets. That adjustment triggered a 4.3% jump in TSMC shares on Friday and pushed the benchmark TAIEX up 2.7%, according to Taiwanese media.Article continues below You may like AMD's market cap hits all-time high, Intel hits 25-year high on Agentic AI's insatiable demand for CPUs Global semiconductor foundry market hit a record $320 billion in 2025 as TSMC pulled further ahead Ongoing trade war has TSMC and Taiwan stuck between a rock and a hard place TSMC reported record first-quarter earnings last week, with net income up 40.6% year-over-year to roughly $18 billion. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to more than 30% growth and confirmed plans to add another 3nm fab to meet AI demand, which it expects to outstrip supply through 2027.South Korea's market, KOSPI, has already overtaken both Germany and France this year and is up 44% in 2026. Samsung and SK hynix account for nearly half the index's total weighting. The former’s share price has nearly quadrupled since the start of 2025, while SK hynix has risen roughly sixfold over the same period. These gains reflect the ongoing memory supercycle, with demand for HBM far exceeding current production capacity.William Bratton, head of cash equity research for Asia-Pacific at BNP Paribas, told the Financial Times that, aside from ASML, European-listed equities have almost no exposure to the current AI hardware buildout. He added that South Korea could overtake the UK as well if the current rally continues.The disparity between these semiconductor-heavy Asian markets and Europe highlights how narrowly the financial benefits of the AI infrastructure boom are distributed. Taiwan's GDP is roughly $977 billion, according to IMF estimates, versus the UK's $4.3 trillion. While the gap between those two economies hasn’t changed, the gap between their stock markets has.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 台湾股市超越英国，尽管经济规模不到英国的四分之一——AI热潮推动台湾前进，台积电一家就占台湾总市值40%以上
history.descriptionZh:
台湾股市市值近日首次超越英国，成为全球资本市场格局变化的一个重要里程碑。根据彭博数据，台湾上市公司总市值已达到约4.3万亿美元，略高于欧洲最大股票市场英国。而这一转变几乎完全由全球对人工智能（AI）芯片的旺盛需求推动。尽管台湾经济规模仅为英国的四分之一（根据IMF估算，台湾GDP约9770亿美元，英国为4.3万亿美元），但其股市市值却已反超。与此同时，韩国股市（KOSPI）正在迅速追赶，目前仅落后约1400亿美元，且因三星电子和SK海力士的强劲表现，差距正在快速缩小。

此次台湾股市的主导力量是台积电（TSMC）。台积电市值约1.98万亿美元，占台湾股市总市值的40%，这一集中度在全球主要经济体中绝无仅有。作为对比，苹果公司在美国标普500指数中的权重仅约7%。本周四，台湾金融监督管理委员会将本地股票基金对单只股票的投资上限从基金净值的10%提高至25%，该调整仅适用于市值权重超过10%的公司——目前只有台积电满足条件。这一政策变化推动台积电股价于周五上涨4.3%，并带动台湾加权指数（TAIEX）上升2.7%。

台积电在上一周公布了创纪录的第一季度财报：净利润同比增长40.6%，达到约180亿美元。公司已将全年营收增长预期上调至超过30%，并确认计划新增一座3纳米晶圆厂以满足AI需求。台积电预计AI芯片需求将持续超出供应能力，至少到2027年。

韩国方面，KOSPI指数今年已超越德国和法国股市，2026年以来累计上涨44%。三星电子和SK海力士合计占该指数权重近一半。前者股价自2025年初以来已上涨近四倍，同期后者涨幅约六倍。这种增长反映了当前的内存超级周期，尤其是高带宽存储器（HBM）的需求远超出当前产能。

法国巴黎银行亚太现金股票研究主管William Bratton对《金融时报》表示，除ASML外，欧洲上市企业几乎未参与当前AI硬件建设的热潮。他补充说，如果目前涨势持续，韩国股市也可能超越英国。这些半导体密集型亚洲市场与欧洲之间的巨大差异，凸显了AI基础设施繁荣带来的金融收益分布极为不均——尽管台湾与英国的经济规模差距未变，但两者股市的市值鸿沟已经逆转。
